events.label: reasoning
events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"staged","component_conditions":["non-compliant"],"percent_complete":0,"value":0,"confidence":0.95,"findings":["Arc-flash PPE rating is below the task category indicated on the arc-flash label (DOC+ID mismatch); staged clothing does not meet required incident energy/cal category for the task."],"actions":["Stop task until arc-rated clothing/PPE matching or exceeding the label's PPE category is staged and verified against the equipment's arc-flash hazard analysis label.","Reconcile the DOC+ID label against the task's arc-flash boundary and incident energy; select appropriate ATPV/ELIM-rated garments, face shield, hood, gloves, and voltage-rated tools.","Conduct a pre-job briefing confirming PPE category match before energized work begins."],"rationale":"The staged PPE has an arc-flash rating below the task category shown on the equipment label, so it cannot provide adequate thermal protection for the anticipated incident energy. NFPA 70E requires arc-rated PPE matched to the hazard analysis category, and OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires PPE appropriate for the identified hazard. Work must not proceed until compliant PPE is provided.","references":["NFPA 70E (Arc-Flash Hazard Analysis and PPE Category/Incident Energy Selection)","OSHA 1910.132 - General PPE Requirements","OSHA 1910.137 - Electrical Protective Equipment"]}

NFPA 70E requires arc-rated PPE matched to the hazard analysis category, and OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires PPE appropriate for the identified hazard. Work must not proceed until compliant PPE is provided.","references":["NFPA 70E (Arc-Flash Hazard Analysis and PPE Category/Incident Energy Selection)","OSHA 1910.132 - General PPE Requirements","OSHA 1910.137 - Electrical Protective Equipment"]}
